operating_state: Total revenue declined 4% to $90.8 billion driven by iPhone weakness across most regions, particularly Greater China (-8%) and Rest of Asia Pacific (-17%). Services revenue grew 14% to $23.9 billion from advertising, App Store and cloud services strength, while Mac revenue increased 4% from laptop demand. iPad revenue fell 17% due to lower Pro and 9th generation sales, and Wearables declined 10% from reduced Wearables and Accessories demand.
cost_structure: Total gross margin expanded to 46.6% from 44.3% as Services margin improved to 74.6% from different mix dynamics while Products margin held steady at 36.6%. Operating expenses increased to 16% of revenue from 14% driven by higher R&D headcount costs and infrastructure-related selling expenses. Services gross margin expansion offset Products volume declines and foreign currency headwinds.
material_events: Board authorized additional $110 billion share repurchase program in May 2024 and raised quarterly dividend to $0.25 from $0.24. Company repurchased $23.5 billion in shares and paid $3.7 billion in dividends during the quarter. Updated MacBook Air 13-inch and 15-inch models were announced in Q2 2024.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency weakness relative to USD created unfavorable impacts across Europe, Greater China, Japan and Rest of Asia Pacific regions. Manufacturing purchase obligations increased to $34.2 billion with $34.1 billion payable within 12 months. Macroeconomic conditions including inflation, interest rates and currency fluctuations continue to directly impact results.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued annual dividend increases subject to Board approval and maintains capital return program execution. Company believes cash balances, operating cash flow and debt market access sufficient for requirements and capital returns over next 12 months and beyond. No specific revenue or margin guidance provided.
uncertainty_profile: Intensified regulatory scrutiny includes EU Digital Markets Act compliance challenges and ongoing antitrust investigations in multiple jurisdictions including US civil lawsuits alleging smartphone market monopolization. Search licensing arrangements with partners face government investigations that could materially affect commercial relationships. New accounting standards for income tax and segment disclosures effective in 2025-2026 require enhanced reporting.
